expectations.text: Flags that the Manager calls InsertAsync / UpdateAsync (i.e. persists), and states this is wrong because domain services must not persist
expectations.evidence: Section '1. Domain services must not persist (Rule 3)': 'It must never call InsertAsync / UpdateAsync / DeleteAsync... Your CreateAsync calls _orderRepository.InsertAsync(order, autoSave: true) and ChangePriceAsync calls _orderRepository.UpdateAsync(order). Both are forbidden in a domain service.'

expectations.text: Recommends switching the Manager's injected repositories to IReadOnlyRepository<T, Guid> (for Customer and Order)
expectations.evidence: Corrected manager declares 'private readonly IReadOnlyRepository<Customer, Guid> _customerRepository;' and 'private readonly IReadOnlyRepository<Order, Guid> _orderRepository;' and the prose: 'A *Manager should inject IReadOnlyRepository<T, TKey> for every repository it needs'

expectations.text: Corrected ChangePriceAsync receives an Order as a parameter from the caller rather than loading it via _orderRepository.GetAsync(orderId) inside the Manager
expectations.evidence: 'public Task ChangePriceAsync(Order order, decimal newPrice) { order.SetPrice(newPrice); return Task.CompletedTask; }' — entity is passed in, no GetAsync inside the manager

expectations.text: Corrected CreateAsync returns the new Order without calling InsertAsync inside the Manager
expectations.evidence: 'return new Order(id, customerId, number); // Manager does NOT persist' inside CreateAsync

expectations.text: Shows an OrderAppService that orchestrates: holds the tracking IRepository<Order, Guid>, mints Id via GuidGenerator, calls the Manager, and persists with InsertAsync/UpdateAsync
expectations.evidence: OrderAppService has 'private readonly IRepository<Order, Guid> _orderRepository; // tracking repo lives HERE', mints id via 'GuidGenerator.Create()', calls '_orderManager.CreateAsync(...)' and '_orderManager.ChangePriceAsync(order, newPrice)', and persists via '_orderRepository.InsertAsync(order)' and '_orderRepository.UpdateAsync(order)'

claims.claim: Domain services must not persist; the application service flushes when its method returns under the same UoW
claims.evidence: Matches ABP DDD guidance — domain services run inside the same UoW; persistence is owned by the application layer

claims.claim: Price-range invariant should live in Order.SetPrice rather than the manager
claims.evidence: Standard DDD guidance — entity-level state-only invariants belong on the aggregate; response also moves the BusinessException into Order.SetPrice

claims.claim: GuidGenerator is available on both ApplicationService and DomainService base classes
claims.evidence: Both base classes expose GuidGenerator via property injection in ABP — accurate

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks that the response surfaces the entity-level invariant move (newPrice <= 0 belongs in Order.SetPrice). Both responses raised it, but a response that left the price guard in the Manager would still pass all 5 assertions.

suggestions.assertion: Corrected CreateAsync returns the new Order without calling InsertAsync inside the Manager
suggestions.reason: Could be strengthened by also asserting that the Manager's CreateAsync accepts the pre-minted Id as a parameter (rather than calling GuidGenerator internally) — this is the conventional ABP pattern and helps discriminate borderline solutions.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted at the core 'Manager should not persist / should not hold tracking repos' lesson and clearly discriminate the two responses.
